wiki.summary: "Headphones On" is the fourth single and closing track on Addison Rae's debut album Addison. It was released on April 18, 2025. The song was first teased in late March through Instagram photos from the music video set. A snippet from the song was then teased on April 10, showing her roller-skating with the song playing. The song features a 90s-inspired trip-hop and R&B-styled production. At the time of release, it was her most introspective and emotionally raw release, described as "confessional. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Headphones On" is the fourth single and closing track on Addison Rae's debut album Addison. It was released on April 18, 2025. The song was first teased in late March through Instagram photos from the music video set. A snippet from the song was then teased on April 10, showing her roller-skating with the song playing. The song features a 90s-inspired trip-hop and R&B-styled production. At the time of release, it was her most introspective and emotionally raw release, described as "confessional." Lyrically, the song addresses personal themes such as identity, emotional escape, and her parents’ divorce. Rae described the process of making the song as "healing, emotional, and extremely spiritual." Critics praised "Headphones On" as a turning point in Rae’s artistry, citing it as a bold, vulnerable closer that deepens her pop persona. Publications like Pitchfork, Slant, and Clash highlighted the song’s atmosphere and emotional weight, with several calling it a standout track and a contender for Song of the Summer. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.
